intent: Redesign trash page file cards so restore and purge actions live on a dedicated button row beneath the card text.
assumptions: Only the trash page card layout changes.
Trash APIs, batch toolbar actions, theme tokens, and backend behavior remain unchanged.
Card titles stay single-line with ellipsis.
Buttons keep stopping propagation so card selection is not toggled by action clicks.
Existing glass button styling and lucide icons remain the visual language.
risks: The trash grid can render very narrow cards, so action labels must not crowd the card or force unstable heights.
Moving actions out of the thumbnail must preserve restore and purge behavior.
Fluid sizing constraints must be preserved for user-visible dimensions.
The current worktree contains unrelated changes, so this task must avoid broad formatting or unrelated files.
steps: Add a failing trash card test that expects a dedicated action row.
Move restore and purge buttons from the thumbnail overlay into the card meta area beneath the retention countdown.
Add scoped card action row/button styling that preserves narrow-grid readability and fluid sizing.
Run the focused trash page test.
Run frontend fluid sizing checks and lint if the focused test passes.
Review the final diff to confirm only the intended trash layout files changed.
